children.text: A 35 year old pilot received a Class 1 medical examination on June 10, 2010 and it was issued on July 14, 2010. The pilot may exercise their private pilot privileges until the

answers: last day of June 2015
last day of June 2011
first day of July 2015
first day of July 2011
correctAnswer: last day of June 2015

children.text: A life preserver, individual flotation device, or personal flotation device for each person on board must be readily available for any flight operating over the water

answers: at more than 100 NM from a suitable landing site or further than 30 minutes at the flight planned cruising speed from a suitable landing site, whichever distance is the lesser
further than 30 minutes at the flight planned cruising speed from a suitable landing site
further than 30 minutes at the flight planned cruising speed from a suitable landing site, and in no case more than 50 NM from shore
beyond a point where the aircraft could reach shore in the event of an engine failure, and in no case more than 50 NM from shore
correctAnswer: beyond a point where the aircraft could reach shore in the event of an engine failure, and in no case more than 50 NM from shore

children.text: A transponder with altitude encoding capability (Mode C) is required for all Class

answers: A, B, and C airspace, and any Class D or E airspace that is designated transponder airspace in the DAH
A, and B airspace, and any Class C or D airspace that is designated transponder airspace in the DAH
A, B, and C airspace, and any airspace that is designated transponder airspace in the DAH
A, B, and C airspace
correctAnswer: A, B, and C airspace, and any Class D or E airspace that is designated transponder airspace in the DAH

children.text: Which statement is false?

answers: Class C airspace may include CZs but not TCAs
An ATC clearance is required for VFR aircraft to enter Class C airspace
An aircraft without two-way radio communication may enter Class C airspace during day VFR conditions if prior authorization has been received
Class D airspace becomes Class E airspace when the appropriate ATC unit is not in operation
correctAnswer: Class C airspace may include CZs but not TCAs

children.text: An arrival report to close a flight itinerary shall be filed as soon as practicable but not later than

answers: 24 hours after the filed estimated time of arrival or the SAR time specified in the flight itinerary
12 hours after the filed estimated time of arrival or the SAR time specified in the flight itinerary
2 hours after the filed estimated time of arrival or the SAR time specified in the flight itinerary
1 hour after the filed estimated time of arrival or the SAR time specified in the flight itinerary
correctAnswer: 24 hours after the filed estimated time of arrival or the SAR time specified in the flight itinerary

children.text: An old style ELT may only be activated for the purpose of testing it during the first 5 minutes of any

answers: UTC hour and for less than 5 seconds
hour and for less than 5 seconds
hour and for less than 10 seconds
UTC hour and for less than 10 seconds
correctAnswer: UTC hour and for less than 5 seconds

children.text: When in VFR flight within controlled airspace a pilot must be at least . . . . . and . . . . . from clouds.

answers: 500 feet below, 1 mile horizontally
1 000 feet below, 1 000 feet horizontally
500 feet below, 1 000 feet horizontally
500 feet below, 2 000 feet horizontally
correctAnswer: 500 feet below, 1 mile horizontally

children.text: Which document is not required to be carried on board an aircraft?

answers: Certificate of Airworthiness
Proof of liability insurance
Weight and balance
Intercept orders
correctAnswer: Intercept orders

children.text: It is true regarding right-of-way that

answers: a single-engine aeroplane is converging upon a glider flying at the same altitude. The glider is to the left of the aeroplane, the aeroplane must yield to the glider
a glider must give way to an airship when converging
when two aircraft are approaching head-on each will alter heading to the left
when an aircraft is overtaking another it has the right-of-way and must pass the other aircraft on its starboard side
correctAnswer: a single-engine aeroplane is converging upon a glider flying at the same altitude. The glider is to the left of the aeroplane, the aeroplane must yield to the glider

children.text: Deviations from ATC instructions and clearances are

answers: not permitted for any reason
permitted for safety reasons as long as ATC is advised as soon as possible for the reason
permitted as long as ATC is advised as soon as possible for the reason
permitted as long as ATC is advised when time permits
correctAnswer: permitted for safety reasons as long as ATC is advised as soon as possible for the reason

children.text: Regarding altimeter setting procedures

answers: the altimeter shall be set prior to takeoff to the current altimeter setting for that aerodrome or if an altimeter setting is not available to the aerodrome elevation.
prior to starting a descent for landing the altimeter shall be set to the altimeter setting of that airport, if available.
while in flight in the Altimeter Setting Region the altimeter shall be set to the altimeter setting of the nearest station along the route of flight.
all of the above are correct.
correctAnswer: all of the above are correct.

children.text: The night definition is?

answers: The period of time during any day that starts at the end of evening civil twilight and ends at the start of morning civil twilight.
Begins when the center of the sun's disc is 6° below the horizon and is ascending, and ends at sunrise, approximately 25 minutes later.
The period of time during any day that begins with the morning civil twilight and ends with the evening civil twilight.
Begins at sunset and ends when the center of the sun's disc is 6° below the horizon and is descending, approximately 45 minutes later.
correctAnswer: The period of time during any day that starts at the end of evening civil twilight and ends at the start of morning civil twilight.

children.text: For a day VFR flight the aeroplane must have enough fuel on board to fly to the destination and then fly for

answers: 45 minutes.
45 minutes at normal cruising speed.
30 minutes at normal cruising speed.
30 minutes.
correctAnswer: 30 minutes at normal cruising speed.

children.text: For VFR in uncontrolled airspace at or above 1 000 feet AGL a pilot must have a flight visibility not less than

answers: 3 miles day or night, and distance from cloud is at least 500 feet vertically and 2 000 feet horizontally.
1 mile day and 3 miles night, and distance from cloud is at least 500 feet vertically and 1 mile horizontally.
1 mile day and 3 miles night, and distance from cloud is at least 500 feet vertically and 2 000 feet horizontally.
2 miles day and 3 miles night, and clear of cloud.
correctAnswer: 1 mile day and 3 miles night, and distance from cloud is at least 500 feet vertically and 2 000 feet horizontally.

children.text: If a pilot is cleared to land and hold short of an intersecting runway, the pilot should

answers: refuse the clearance.
read back the clearance and comply.
comply with the clearance.
advise that all of the runway is required.
correctAnswer: read back the clearance and comply.

children.text: If the runway at an airport was closed the pilot would find the information by

answers: reading NOTAMs.
listening to the ATIS.
reading the CFS.
phoning ahead to the airport operator.
correctAnswer: reading NOTAMs.

children.text: Completion of . . . . . would meet the requirements for a recurrent training program.

answers: a flight review with an instructor
a Transport Canada safety seminar
a written examination for a license
any of the above
correctAnswer: any of the above

children.text: Special VFR may be requested and authorized when the aeroplane can be operated with a flight visibility not less than

answers: 1 mile and at night the authorization is for the purpose of allowing the aeroplane to only land.
1 mile.
1 mile, radio equipped, and at night the authorization is for the purpose of allowing the aeroplane to only land.
1 mile, radio equipped, and the authorization is for the purpose of allowing the aircraft to only land.
correctAnswer: 1 mile, radio equipped, and at night the authorization is for the purpose of allowing the aeroplane to only land.

children.text: When departing the circuit at an uncontrolled airport the runway heading should be maintained until

answers: circuit altitude and then a turn should be initiated to the en route heading.
500 feet and then a turn should be initiated to the en route heading.
1 000 feet and then a turn should be initiated to the en route heading.
clear of the zone.
correctAnswer: circuit altitude and then a turn should be initiated to the en route heading.

children.text: Except while taking off or landing no aircraft shall be flown at an altitude of less than . . . . . from any person, vehicle, or structure in non built-up area.

answers: 500 feet
2 000 feet
1 000 feet
300 feet
correctAnswer: 500 feet

children.text: The altimeter shall be set to . . . . . in the altimeter setting region.

answers: the altimeter setting of the nearest reporting station en route
29.92 in Hg
the station pressure
the destination airport's setting on departure
correctAnswer: the altimeter setting of the nearest reporting station en route

children.text: If an ATF frequency is not listed the pilot should use
No person shall drop an object from an aircraft in flight unless

answers: it does not cause or create a hazard to any person or property on the ground.
they receive a permit from TC.
not conducted in Class F airspace.
not conducted in Class C or D airspace.
correctAnswer: it does not cause or create a hazard to any person or property on the ground.

children.text: No person shall displace, move or interfere with an aircraft involved in an accident, or the components or contents of any such aircraft, or interfere with or otherwise disrupt an occurrence site

answers: without first having obtained permission to do so from an investigator.
except to extricate any person or to prevent destruction by fire.
except to avoid danger to any person or property.
for any of the above.
correctAnswer: for any of the above.

children.text: Not required to be carried on board an aircraft is the

answers: certificate of insurance.
journey log.
intercept orders.
certificate of registration.
correctAnswer: intercept orders.

children.text: A VFR flight plan or itinerary must be filed

answers: at all times.
when flying more than 25 NM from the departure aerodrome.
when flying more than 50 NM from the departure aerodrome.
when landing and shutting down at an airport other than the point of departure.
correctAnswer: when flying more than 25 NM from the departure aerodrome.

children.text: An aircraft on the ground sees green flashes from the tower which means

answers: cleared for takeoff.
cleared to taxi.
hold current position.
cleared to the circuit.
correctAnswer: cleared to taxi.

children.text: An aircraft is flying in the SDA on a magnetic track of 185° with a 12° wind correction to the left. The correct VFR cruising altitude would be

answers: 4 500 feet.
5 500 feet.
4 000 feet.
5 000 feet.
correctAnswer: 4 500 feet.

children.text: For a VFR flight in controlled airspace the minimum distance from cloud is

answers: 3 SM.
500 feet vertically and 1 mile horizontally.
500 feet vertically and 2 000 feet horizontally.
500 feet vertically and 3 miles horizontally.
correctAnswer: 500 feet vertically and 1 mile horizontally.

children.text: A runway used at night must have a line of fixed white lights on each side. These lights must be visible in all directions for at least

answers: 2 NM.
3 NM.
4 NM.
5 NM.
correctAnswer: 2 NM.

children.text: The maximum distance that adjacent runway lights can be spaced is

answers: 20 meters.
200 feet.
300 feet.
150 feet.
correctAnswer: 200 feet.

children.text: For an aircraft flying VFR in uncontrolled airspace at 2 500 feet AGL the minimum visibility is

answers: 1 mile during the day.
1 mile during the night.
3 miles during the day.
2 miles during the night.
correctAnswer: 1 mile during the day.

children.text: No person shall act as a crew member of an aircraft within . . . . . of consuming an alcoholic beverage.

answers: 36 hours
8 hours
12 hours
24 hours
correctAnswer: 12 hours

children.text: Except for the purpose of taking off or landing, no person shall operate an aeroplane less than . . . . . above the highest obstacle located within a . . . . . horizontal distance.

answers: 1 000 feet, 2 000 feet
1 000 feet, 1 mile
2 000 feet, 1 mile
1 000 feet, 4 000 feet
correctAnswer: 1 000 feet, 2 000 feet

children.text: No person shall operate an aircraft below 3 000 feet AGL and within 10 NM of a controlled airport at an indicated speed greater than
When departing from an airport in the standard pressure region set the altimeter to

answers: 29.92 in Hg just prior to reaching the desired flight level.
29.92 in Hg just after reaching the desired flight level.
29.92 in Hg prior to takeoff.
the local altimeter setting.
correctAnswer: 29.92 in Hg just prior to reaching the desired flight level.

children.text: The minimum fuel requirements for day VFR in an aeroplane is enough fuel to fly to the destination then

answers: for 30 minutes after.
for 45 minutes after.
for 60 minutes after.
to an alternate and then for 30 minutes after that.
correctAnswer: for 30 minutes after.

children.text: In order to act as pilot-in-command at night with passengers the

answers: aircraft must be equipped with a functioning landing light.
aircraft must be equipped with a functioning landing light and the PIC must have completed 6 takeoffs and landings in the previous 6 months.
aircraft must be equipped with a functioning landing light and the PIC must have completed 5 takeoffs and landings in the previous 6 months.
PIC must have completed 5 takeoffs and landings in the previous 6 months.
correctAnswer: aircraft must be equipped with a functioning landing light and the PIC must have completed 5 takeoffs and landings in the previous 6 months.

children.text: A flight crew member's safety belt must be fastened

answers: only during takeoff and landing when passengers are on board the aircraft.
only during takeoff and landing.
while the crew member is at their stations.
only when there is turbulence.
correctAnswer: while the crew member is at their stations.

children.text: A glider and an aeroplane at the same altitude or approximately so are approaching each other head on, the action that should be taken is

answers: both aircraft should immediately alter heading to the right.
the aeroplane should give way because the glider has the right-of-way.
the aeroplane should give way because it is more maneuverable.
both aircraft must alter heading to the left.
correctAnswer: both aircraft should immediately alter heading to the right.

children.text: A portable electronic device should not be used on board an aircraft

answers: when it is included in the MEL.
when it is not included in the MEL.
if it may impair the functioning of any aircraft systems.
at anytime.
correctAnswer: if it may impair the functioning of any aircraft systems.

children.text: It is correct in regards to advisory airspace that aircraft

answers: must have a transponder to enter.
must have a communication radio to enter.
with only a flight instructor on board are permitted to enter.
that are not participating in the airspace are encouraged to avoid flight in advisory airspace during periods specified on the VNC/VTA and in NOTAMs.
correctAnswer: that are not participating in the airspace are encouraged to avoid flight in advisory airspace during periods specified on the VNC/VTA and in NOTAMs.

children.text: A pilot flying a single-engine aeroplane observes a multi-engine aeroplane approaching from the left. Which pilot should give way?

answers: The pilot of the single-engine aeroplane should give way as the other aeroplane is to the left.
Each pilot should alter course to the right.
The pilot of the multi-engine aeroplane should give way as the single-engine aeroplane is to its right.
The pilot of the single-engine aeroplane should give way to the multi-engine aeroplane because it is faster.
correctAnswer: The pilot of the multi-engine aeroplane should give way as the single-engine aeroplane is to its right.

children.text: A sufficient supply of supplemental oxygen must be available for all flight crew members for the entire duration of flight at cabin pressure altitudes above . . . . . but not exceeding . . . . . when flying more than 30 minutes.

answers: 10 000 feet ASL, 13 000 feet ASL
12 000 feet MSL, 15 000 feet MSL
10 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude, 13 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude
12 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude, 15 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude
correctAnswer: 10 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude, 13 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude

children.text: Aeroplane A is overtaking aeroplane B. Which aeroplane has the right-of-way?

answers: Aeroplane A and the pilot should alter course to the right to pass.
Airplane B and the pilot should expect to be passed on the right.
Airplane B and the pilot should expect to be passed on the left.
No aircraft should ever overtake another aircraft.
correctAnswer: Airplane B and the pilot should expect to be passed on the right.

children.text: An aeroplane and an airship are converging. If the airship is left of the aeroplane's position

answers: the airship has the right of way.
each aircraft should alter course to the right.
the aeroplane has the right of way.
none of the above apply.
correctAnswer: the airship has the right of way.

children.text: An aeroplane is overtaking a helicopter. Which aircraft has the right-of-way?

answers: Aeroplane and the aeroplane pilot should alter course to the right to pass.
Helicopter and the pilot should expect to be passed on the left.
Helicopter and the pilot should expect to be passed on the right.
Helicopter and the pilot should expect to be passed over top.
correctAnswer: Helicopter and the pilot should expect to be passed on the right.

children.text: Regarding CYA airspace

answers: IFR aircraft are permitted.
non-participating aircraft are encouraged to avoid.
SVFR are permitted.
ATC has authority of CYA areas but not CYR areas.
correctAnswer: non-participating aircraft are encouraged to avoid.

children.text: Flight through a Class F restricted area may not be continued unless the pilot has

answers: received prior authorization from the controlling agency.
filed an IFR flight plan.
received prior permission from the commanding officer of the nearest military base.
filed a Defense VFR flight plan.
correctAnswer: received prior authorization from the controlling agency.

children.text: After receiving general anesthetics a pilot cannot fly

answers: for 48 hours.
until your aviation doctor says it is okay.
for 24 hours.
until you do not feel any lingering effects of the drugs.
correctAnswer: until your aviation doctor says it is okay.

children.text: On a night flight the pilot of aircraft A observes only the green wingtip light of aircraft B. The aircraft are converging and the aircraft that has the right-of-way is

answers: aircraft A and is to the left of aircraft B.
aircraft A and it is to the right of aircraft B.
aircraft B and it is to the right of aircraft A.
none of the above.
correctAnswer: aircraft A and it is to the right of aircraft B.

children.text: VFR flights are prohibited in Class

answers: A
B
C
F
correctAnswer: A

children.text: The Canadian Aviation Regulations stipulate that Canadian registered civil aeroplanes require during takeoff and landing that a seat belt and shoulder harness (if installed) must be properly secured for

answers: flight and cabin crew members
flight crew member(s) only
all persons on board
the PIC
correctAnswer: all persons on board

children.text: Two aircraft of the same category are approaching an airport for the purpose of landing. The right-of-way belongs to the aircraft

answers: at the lower altitude, but the pilot shall not take advantage of this rule to cut in front of or to overtake the other aircraft
that is more maneuverable, and that aircraft may with caution move in front of or overtake the other aircraft
at the higher altitude
at the higher altitude, the aircraft at lower altitude must do a 360° turn to allow the higher aircraft to land
correctAnswer: at the lower altitude, but the pilot shall not take advantage of this rule to cut in front of or to overtake the other aircraft

children.text: Required when cruising at 5 500 feet MSL in the standard pressure region is the altimeter setting of

answers: the departure or destination aerodrome
the nearest station along the route
29.92 in Hg
the current altimeter setting for the route being flown
correctAnswer: 29.92 in Hg

children.text: The minimum oxygen requirements while operating at cabin pressure altitudes in excess of 13 000 feet cabin-pressure-altitude is

answers: a sufficient supply for the flight crew and passengers for the entire duration of the flight at that cabin pressure
not a requirement for balloons at any altitude
that oxygen must be available for the flight crew
the flight crew only must be provided with supplemental oxygen
correctAnswer: a sufficient supply for the flight crew and passengers for the entire duration of the flight at that cabin pressure

children.text: The required calls when entering a mandatory frequency area for the purpose of landing are to call

answers: 5 minutes before entering the area and when clear of the runway
5 miles before entering the area, when joining the circuit, on final approach, and when clear of the runway
10 minutes before entering the area
5 minutes before entering the area, when joining the circuit, on final approach, and when clear of the runway
correctAnswer: 5 minutes before entering the area, when joining the circuit, on final approach, and when clear of the runway

children.text: The required minimum for a VFR flight in uncontrolled airspace at 5 000 feet AGL is

answers: 1 mile visibility, 2 000 feet horizontally and 500 feet vertically from clouds
3 miles visibility, 1 mile horizontally and 500 feet vertically from clouds, and 500 feet AGL
3 miles visibility, 1 mile horizontally and 500 feet vertically from clouds
2 miles visibility and clear of clouds
correctAnswer: 1 mile visibility, 2 000 feet horizontally and 500 feet vertically from clouds

children.text: The minimum fuel required for a VFR flight at night in an airplane is enough to fly to the

answers: first point of intended landing and to fly after that for 20 minutes at normal cruise speed
point of intended landing and to fly after that for 45 minutes at normal cruise speed
first point of intended landing and to fly after that for 30 minutes at normal cruise speed
destination airport and then enough fuel to fly to an alternate airport and then for a period of 45 minutes
correctAnswer: point of intended landing and to fly after that for 45 minutes at normal cruise speed

children.text: An airway starts at

answers: 2 200 feet AGL and is Class E
2 200 feet AGL and is Class G
the surface and is Class E
the surface and is Class G
correctAnswer: 2 200 feet AGL and is Class E

children.text: After scuba diving requiring decompression stops a person should wait

answers: 24 hours
48 hours
12 hours
8 hours
correctAnswer: 24 hours

children.text: In a Class E control zone without an operational FSS the minimum VFR weather requirements would be

answers: 1 mile visibility and clear of cloud.
2 miles visibility and clear of cloud.
3 miles visibility and a ceiling of 1 000 feet AGL or higher.
CAVOK.
correctAnswer: 3 miles visibility and a ceiling of 1 000 feet AGL or higher.

children.text: No person shall fly or attempt to fly as a flight crew member of an aircraft if that person

answers: is less than 18 years of age.
has consumed alcohol within the 24 hour period prior to takeoff.
is aware of being under a physical disability that might render that person unable to meet the requirements for the issue or renewal of their licence or permit.
has received a local anaesthetic within the previous 48 hours.
correctAnswer: is aware of being under a physical disability that might render that person unable to meet the requirements for the issue or renewal of their licence or permit.

children.text: Canadian low level airways are considered . . . . . airspace and are based at . . . . .

answers: Class G, at the surface.
Class B, 12 500 feet ASL.
Class E, 2 200 feet ASL.
Class E, 2 200 feet AGL.
correctAnswer: Class E, 2 200 feet AGL.

children.text: Flight within Class F airspace CYR is

answers: permitted but non participating aircraft are encouraged to avoid.
restricted to TC authorized aircraft.
permitted with prior permission.
prohibited.
correctAnswer: permitted with prior permission.

children.text: While flying within a radar service area the controller states "CESSNA GPTO, TRAFFIC 10 O'CLOCK 3 MILES, OPPOSITE DIRECTION, ALTITUDE UNKNOWN." You should look for the other aircraft between the

answers: right wing and the tail.
right wing and the nose.
left wing and the nose.
left wing and the tail.
correctAnswer: left wing and the nose.

children.text: Air Traffic Control may authorize an aeroplane equipped with a two-way radio to depart from an aerodrome within a control zone during day special VFR provided that the visibility is at least

answers: 2 miles.
1 mile.
1/2 mile.
3 miles.
correctAnswer: 1 mile.

children.text: Which statement is correct?

answers: Gliders are considered aeroplanes
Balloons are aircraft
An aircraft is an aeroplane
An aircraft is a helicopter
correctAnswer: Balloons are aircraft

children.text: When operating in accordance with VFR the aircraft shall be flown

answers: outside designated airways and air routes.
outside control zones.
in three miles or better visibility.
with visual reference to the ground or water.
correctAnswer: with visual reference to the ground or water.

children.text: It is the pilot's responsibility to ascertain that on board the aircraft is the

answers: certificate of airworthiness, certificate of registration, pilot's operating handbook, and journey logbook.
certificate of registration, journey logbook, weight and balance information, certificate of airworthiness, crew licences, insurance documents, and pilot's operating handbook.
certificate of registration, pilot's operating handbook, technical logbook, and aircraft radio licence.
certificate of airworthiness, certificate of registration, pilot's operating handbook, personal logbook, and insurance.
correctAnswer: certificate of registration, journey logbook, weight and balance information, certificate of airworthiness, crew licences, insurance documents, and pilot's operating handbook.

children.text: While on final approach a flashing red light signal from the control tower means

answers: overshoot and go-around.
airport unsafe, do not land.
return for landing.
cleared to land.
correctAnswer: airport unsafe, do not land.

children.text: While operating VFR below 12 500 feet ASL a mode C transponder equipped aircraft should have the transponder code

answers: 1200 with mode selector set to ON.
1200 with mode selector set to ALT.
1400 with mode selector set to ON.
1400 with mode selector set to ALT.
correctAnswer: 1200 with mode selector set to ALT.

children.text: When two aircraft are converging at approximately the same altitude the aircraft that has the other on its right shall give way except for

answers: power-driven heavier-than-air aircraft shall give way to rotary wing aircraft.
helicopters shall give way to aeroplanes.
gliders shall give way to aeroplanes.
power-driven heavier-than-air aircraft shall give way to airships, gliders, and balloons.
correctAnswer: power-driven heavier-than-air aircraft shall give way to airships, gliders, and balloons.

children.text: The amount of fuel and oil carried on board any aeroplane, except an ultra-light, at the commencement of any day VFR flight shall be sufficient for anticipated wind and other weather conditions having been considered to fly

answers: from point of departure to destination at minimum cruising speed.
to the destination and thereafter for 45 minutes at normal cruising speed.
to the destination and thereafter for 30 minutes at normal cruising speed.
to the destination, then to a specified alternate, and thereafter for 45 minutes at normal cruising speed.
correctAnswer: to the destination and thereafter for 30 minutes at normal cruising speed.

children.text: What is the international emergency frequency?

Except as provided by the CARs, unless taking off, landing or attempting to land, no person shall fly an aeroplane over a built-up area or over any open air assembly of persons unless the aeroplane is operated at an altitude from which, in the event of an emergency necessitating an immediate landing, it would be possible to land the aeroplane without creating a hazard to persons or property on the surface, and, in any case, at an altitude that is not lower than . . . . . above the highest obstacle within a radius of . . . . . from the aeroplane.

answers: 500 feet, 500 feet
1 000 feet, 2 000 feet
2 000 feet, 1 000 feet
1 000 feet, 500 feet
correctAnswer: 1 000 feet, 2 000 feet

children.text: In southern domestic airspace a runway is numbered according to its . . . . . bearing and a runway numbered 01 would have a bearing of . . . . .

answers: true, 100°.
magnetic, 100°.
true, 010°.
magnetic, 010°.
correctAnswer: magnetic, 010°.

children.text: In the event of a communications failure while in controlled airspace a pilot should select the code . . . . . on the transponder to alert ATC.

The recency requirements for the holder of a flight crew licence to carry passengers at night is . . . . . night takeoffs and landings within the previous . . . . . months in the same category and class of aircraft used for the flight.
Which flight instruments are required for a power-driven aircraft during day VFR flight?
With regard to a flight itinerary the responsible person means someone who?

answers: has agreed to report the non-arrival of the flight.
is 18 years of age or over.
holds an aeronautical licence.
has agreed to report the arrival of the flight.
correctAnswer: has agreed to report the non-arrival of the flight.

children.text: In an emergency requiring the use of an ELT it should be turned on?

answers: during daylight hours only to conserve the battery.
for the first five minutes of each hour only.
at the ETA of the flight plan.
immediately and left on.
correctAnswer: immediately and left on.

children.text: The minimum lighting allowed to mark takeoff and landing areas at an aerodrome at night is?

answers: one line of fixed white lights.
one line of retro-reflective markers and runway threshold end lights.

two parallel lines of retro-reflective markers with a fixed white light at each end of the runway or two parallel lines of fixed white lights or retro-reflective markers and runway threshold end lights.
a single row of lights from the headlights of cars.
correctAnswer: two parallel lines of retro-reflective markers with a fixed white light at each end of the runway or two parallel lines of fixed white lights or retro-reflective markers and runway threshold end lights.

children.text: For planning a two hour VFR cross-country flight eastbound the highest cruising altitude that can be flown without oxygen being available is?

answers: 9 500 feet ASL.
11 500 feet ASL.
13 000 feet ASL.
13 500 feet ASL.
correctAnswer: 9 500 feet ASL.

children.text: A pilot may commence a flight in an aircraft when assured that frost, ice, or snow?

answers: is not adhering to the leading edge of the wings.
adhering to the critical surfaces will slide off on takeoff.
is not adhering to any of its critical surfaces.
is not adhering to the lifting surfaces of the wing.
correctAnswer: is not adhering to any of its critical surfaces.

children.text: Passenger safety belts must be fastened?

answers: at all times during flight.
during takeoff and landing only.
during taxiing, takeoff and landing only.
during taxiing, takeoff, landing and when directed by the PIC during flight.
correctAnswer: during taxiing, takeoff, landing and when directed by the PIC during flight.

children.text: The controller suggests a takeoff from a runway intersection. A pilot must be aware that?

answers: the remaining runway length will not be stated by the controller.
it is the pilot's responsibility to ensure that the remaining runway length is sufficient for takeoff.
the controller will ensure that the remaining runway length is sufficient for takeoff.
noise abatement procedures have been cancelled.
correctAnswer: it is the pilot's responsibility to ensure that the remaining runway length is sufficient for takeoff.

children.text: A VFR flight departs an airport that requires the aircraft to have a functioning transponder with Mode C reporting capability. Unless otherwise instructed by ATC on departure the transponder should be set to?

answers: STANDBY.
code 1000 mode C—ON.
code 1400 mode C—OFF.
code 1200 mode C—ON.
correctAnswer: code 1200 mode C—ON.

children.text: A pilot after accepting a clearance and subsequently finding that all or part of the clearance cannot be complied with should

answers: disregard the clearance
comply with only the part that is suitable
comply as best as possible under the circumstances
comply as best as possible under the circumstances and advise ATC as soon as possible
correctAnswer: comply as best as possible under the circumstances and advise ATC as soon as possible

children.text: An aircraft cruising VFR in level flight above 3 000 feet AGL on a track of 290°M in the SDA shall be flown at an

answers: even thousand foot altitude
even thousand plus 500 foot altitude
odd thousand foot altitude
odd thousand plus 500 foot altitude
correctAnswer: even thousand plus 500 foot altitude

children.text: Over non-populous areas or over open water a pilot may not fly an aircraft at a distance of less than . . . . . feet from any person, vessel, vehicle or structure.
When the tower associated with a Class C control zone is closed it

answers: becomes Class E uncontrolled
becomes Class E controlled
reverts to Class G
remains Class C
correctAnswer: becomes Class E controlled

children.text: Flight within Class F CYR airspace

answers: may be permitted subject to prior permission
may be undertaken at the pilot's discretion
is prohibited at all times
is permitted if a DVFR flight plan is filed
correctAnswer: may be permitted subject to prior permission

children.text: During VFR flight within controlled airspace the pilot must maintain

answers: at least 500 feet vertically and 2 000 feet horizontally from cloud
at least 500 feet vertically and 1 mile horizontally from cloud
at least 1 000 feet vertically and 1 mile horizontally from cloud
clear of cloud
correctAnswer: at least 500 feet vertically and 1 mile horizontally from cloud

children.text: Pilots when flying over a national park are required to fly at no less than

answers: 1 000 feet AGL
2 000 feet AGL
3 000 feet AGL
2 200 feet AGL
correctAnswer: 2 000 feet AGL

children.text: When flying VFR below 1 000 feet AGL within uncontrolled airspace the aircraft must remain

answers: at least 2 000 feet horizontally and 500 feet vertically from cloud
at least 1 mile horizontally and 500 feet vertically from cloud
at least 2 miles horizontally and 500 feet vertically from cloud
clear of cloud
correctAnswer: clear of cloud

children.text: A large white X placed near the threshold of a runway indicates that the

answers: runway is closed
airport is closed
runway is a hard surface
threshold is displaced
correctAnswer: runway is closed

children.text: Except for balloons and as provided by the CARs no person shall cause any aircraft to takeoff or attempt to takeoff from, land on or attempt to land on any surface within a built-up area of any city or town unless

answers: the aircraft is multi-engined
all obstacles on approach and departure can be cleared by a minimum of 500 feet
that surface is an airport or military aerodrome
noise abatement procedures are followed
correctAnswer: that surface is an airport or military aerodrome

children.text: A single-engine aircraft operating beyond gliding distance from shore or taking off from or landing on water shall have on board

answers: one life jacket for each person.
a water activated ELT.
a life raft of sufficient size to accommodate all persons.
all of the above plus emergency equipment specified in the CARs.
correctAnswer: one life jacket for each person.

children.text: A flight crew member aware of a physical disability that might invalidate the license or permit issue or renewal shall

answers: not exercise their privileges unless authorized by the Minister.
commence a flight.
forward the licence or permit to the Minister.
complete the itinerary and then seek medical advice.
correctAnswer: not exercise their privileges unless authorized by the Minister.

children.text: Over a built-up area a pilot may not fly an aeroplane at a distance less than . . . . . feet above the highest obstacle.
ATC may authorize an aeroplane equipped with a functioning two-way radio to depart from an aerodrome within a control zone under day SVFR provided the visibility is not less than

answers: 1 mile.
2 miles.
3 miles.
1/2 mile.
correctAnswer: 1 mile.

children.text: When over flying an uncontrolled airport to determine the winds an aircraft should be

answers: 2 000 feet above the circuit.
500 feet above circuit height.
2 000 feet above the airport.
any altitude that is clear of the circuit.
correctAnswer: 500 feet above circuit height.

children.text: No person shall exercise the privileges of a license or rating unless that person has acted as pilot-in-command

answers: five times within the previous six months.
five times within the previous 24 months.
at least once within the previous five years.
at least once within the previous two years.
correctAnswer: at least once within the previous five years.

children.text: An old style emergency locator transmitter (ELT) may be tested

answers: during the first 5 minutes after any UTC hour.
during the first 5 minutes after any hour.
anytime.
never.
correctAnswer: during the first 5 minutes after any UTC hour.

children.text: The recommended procedure to ensure that the emergency locator transmitter (ELT) has not been activated is

answers: to ask the airport's control tower if they are receiving an ELT signal.
to monitor 121.5 MHz before engine shutdown.
to turn OFF the aircraft's ELT after landing.
none of the above.
correctAnswer: to monitor 121.5 MHz before engine shutdown.

children.text: When activated an emergency locator transmitter (ELT) transmits on
